properties.remark: PUBLIC MEASURED 62 MPH GUST VIA PERSONAL WEATHER STATION.
MEASURED AT DETROIT RIVER LIGHT.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN DUNDEE, CONFIRMED VIA NWS STORM SURVEY. WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH, WITH PATH WIDTH OF 75 YARDS AND PATH LENGTH OF 7.3 MILES. TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FROM 1103 AM EDT TO 1108 AM EDT. DAMAGE WAS MOST CONCENTRATED IN DOWNTOWN DUNDEE WITH A ROOF BEING PARTIALLY BLOWN OFF, WINDOWS BLOWN OUT, DOWNED LIMBS, AND NEARBY CAR DAMAGE.
AMATEUR RADIO OPERATOR REPORTS NUMEROUS WIRES DOWN, PARTIAL APARTMENT BUILDING ROOF DESTROYED, AND DAMAGE TO GAZEBO IN DOWNTOWN DUNDEE.
NUMEROUS TREES AND WIRES DOWN REPORTED ACROSS NORTHEAST PORTIONS OF MONROE COUNTY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

Many trees snapped and uprooted due to straight line winds from Wallace Road to Cedar Road, along and north of West Van Orgen Rd. Estimated wind speeds up to 90 mph.

An EF-2 tornado over northeast Ingham county weakened and tracked into Livingston county near Fowlerville, producing EF-1 damage, with winds up to 90 MPH. Storm track length in Livingston county was 1.3 miles.
Near Fenton Rd and North Long Lake Rd, 6 inch diameter tree branches down in the road.

A confirmed tornado, causing EF1 damage with winds up to 90 mph entered Livingston county near West Branch Red Cedar River, crossing Wallace Rd. Tornado tracked close to the river through Cedar Rd and reached at least close to North Nicholson Rd near I-96. The storm survey is still ongoing and more details of the tornado will be issued later today in a PNS.
Public reports multiple 6 to 12 inch diameter limbs down south of Fowlerville at Nicholson Road between Grand River and Van Orden via social media.

1 foot diameter tree reported down.
Trees and power lines reported down across Salem
Confirmed EF-0 tornado in Canton producing winds up to 80 mph. Path length was 1.7 miles.

The weak tornado touched down just northwest of Pheasant Run Golf course and track southeast. Dozens of thrrees were downed and uprooted. The tornado continued southeast, tracking across Summit Parkway and Canton Circle Road, bringing down large limbs in the process. As the tornado reached Sheldon Road, trees were sheared and a hardwood tree fell onto a house. The tornado dissipated just north of the Lower River Rouge.
Large tree blown onto road.
25 foot pine tree was uprooted and landed on neighbor's roof in neighborhood near Sheldon Rd and Palmer Rd.

Dispatch reports trees and power lines down across the county. Numerous reports came from around Clinton Township.
Multiple trees uprooted, shingles off roofs.
Confirmed EF-1 tornado in Belleville, with maximum winds of 90 mph and a path length of 3 miles.

The weak tornado touched down near the Wagner Homestead Farm with mainly leaf and twigs blown down. The tornado tracked southeast and crossed Elwell road near Belmont Drive. Three were downed and uprooted. The tornado hit the Mobile Manor Trail Park. A tree was broken in half with uprooted softwoods noted. More significant tree damage was noted as the tornado approached Martinsville Road, generally tracking close to the Hubbard Drain. Trees down and siding peeled offr some homes, along with a la

MEASURED AT DETROIT METRO AIRPORT.

A weak tornado touched down in a farm field in Ash Township north of Newport Road and half a mile west of North Telegraph Road. The tornado tracked southeast and hit Frenchtown Villa Mobile Home Park where the bullk of the damage occurred on the north side of the park. Roofs were torn off and a manufactured home was flipped over. Other damage included shingle and roof damage on the southeast side of the park. The tornado continued to track southeast and crossed over I-75 by the Meijer Distributi
